REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2334 46th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94116)
Off Calendar Jan-12-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Quane appeared in person for and with Deborah Hunt by zoom.
Justin Hedemark appeared in person for and with James Kirk (beneficiary) in person.

No one appeared to place an overbid on the property. The sale was confirmed at $1,008,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale with a commission of 3.5% to the seller's agent, and no commission to the buyer's agent because the buyer is the agent. The Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property is under submission (subject to review of the order). An additional bond is not required.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-01-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Deborah Hunt in propria persona (Petitioner)
Also present: Michael Hunt (petitioner's spouse) and Ted Kirk (Petitioner's brother)

The Court inquired whether Ms. Hunt has experience with administering an estate and why she did not hire an attorney. Ms. Hunt stated she does not have experience with administering an estate, she is using a manual, that it is a straightforward accounting with plenty of assets, did not want to spend $28,000.00 for attorney's fees, knows to file Letters, pay the property tax, that her brother has lived in the property for 18 years and wants to continue living in the property, and the siblings have agreed to divide the bonds and stocks. Mr. Hunt stated that the siblings agreed to: allow Ted Kirk to live in the property and take care of the bills, possibly rent out the upstairs to help with property tax, divide the checking/savings accounts, and take care of anything i.e. a new roof. The Court stated that the siblings need to agree, and things don't always end up easy, the Administrator needs to act in the best interest of the estate, do accountings, keep track of receipts in case anyone complains, encourages petitioner to obtain an attorney, will require bond, property cannot be sold without court approval, and funds cannot be taken out of the estate without court approval. Ms. Hunt stated that she requested limited authority so she would not have to obtain a bond, and if the Court is requiring a bond, she would like full authority. The Court noted bond will be required for $500,000.00 if limited authority or $1.5million if full authority. Mr. Hunt stated they have no intentions of selling anytime soon. Ms. Hunt stated that her husband was a supply sergeant in the army and did her mom's taxes for the last 10 years. The Petition for Letters of Administration is Taken Under Submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
